categories.name: أَرْكَانُ النَّجَاةِ فِي سُورَةِ الْعَصْرِ
categories.nameEn: Pillars of Salvation in Surah Al-Asr

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا۟ لَكُمْ فَٱخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَـٰنًۭا وَقَالُوا۟ حَسْبُنَا ٱللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ ٱلْوَكِيلُ
items.verseEn: Those to whom people [i.e., hypocrites] said, "Indeed, the people have gathered against you, so fear them." But it [merely] increased them in faith, and they said, "Sufficient for us is Allāh, and [He is] the best Disposer of affairs."

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: But those who believed and did righteous deeds - We charge no soul except [within] its capacity. Those are the companions of Paradise; they will abide therein eternally.

items.verse: ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ
items.verseEn: The truth is from your Lord, so never be among the doubters.

items.verse: وَمَا تَنقِمُ مِنَّآ إِلَّآ أَنْ ءَامَنَّا بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّنَا لَمَّا جَآءَتْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَآ أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًۭا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And you do not resent us except because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour upon us patience and let us die as Muslims [in submission to You]."

categories.name: أَصْنَافُ الْمُحْتَاجِينَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Categories of the Needy in the Quran

items.verseEn: Or a needy person in misery
And as for the petitioner, do not repel [him].
For the petitioner and the deprived -

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْ هَـٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدَ ءَامِنًۭا وَٱجْنُبْنِى وَبَنِىَّ أَن نَّعْبُدَ ٱلْأَصْنَامَ
items.verseEn: And [mention, O Muḥammad], when Abraham said, "My Lord, make this city [i.e., Makkah] secure and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.

items.verse: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِيلُ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنتُمْ لَهَا عَـٰكِفُونَ
items.verseEn: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted?"

items.verse: لَآ إِكْرَاهَ فِى ٱلدِّينِ ۖ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ ٱلرُّشْدُ مِنَ ٱلْغَىِّ ۚ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدِ ٱسْتَمْسَكَ بِٱلْعُرْوَةِ ٱلْوُثْقَىٰ لَا ٱنفِصَامَ لَهَا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
items.verseEn: There shall be no compulsion in [acceptance of] the religion. The right course has become distinct from the wrong. So whoever disbelieves in ṭāghūt and believes in Allāh has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no break in it. And Allāh is Hearing and Knowing.

items.verse: يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًۭا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍۢ يُوفِضُونَ
items.verseEn: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

categories.name: الْغَيْبِيَّاتُ
categories.nameEn: Aspects of the Unseen Known Only to Allah

items.verse: يَسْـَٔلُكَ ٱلنَّاسُ عَنِ ٱلسَّاعَةِ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ تَكُونُ قَرِيبًا
items.verseEn: People ask you concerning the Hour. Say, "Knowledge of it is only with Allāh. And what may make you perceive? Perhaps the Hour is near."

items.verse: ۞ وَعِندَهُۥ مَفَاتِحُ ٱلْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍۢ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍۢ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: And with Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him. And He knows what is on the land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls but that He knows it. And no grain is there within the darknesses of the earth and no moist or dry [thing] but that it is [written] in a clear record.

items.verse: ٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَحْمِلُ كُلُّ أُنثَىٰ وَمَا تَغِيضُ ٱلْأَرْحَامُ وَمَا تَزْدَادُ ۖ وَكُلُّ شَىْءٍ عِندَهُۥ بِمِقْدَارٍ
items.verseEn: Allāh knows what every female carries and what the wombs lose [prematurely] or exceed. And everything with Him is by due measure.

items.verse: كُلُّ نَفْسٍۢ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۗ وَنَبْلُوكُم بِٱلشَّرِّ وَٱلْخَيْرِ فِتْنَةًۭ ۖ وَإِلَيْنَا تُرْجَعُونَ
items.verseEn: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.

harf.hint: The unseen realm known only to Allah
harf.arabicVerse: قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ عَـٰلِمَ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ أَنتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبَادِكَ فِى مَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
harf.verse: 39:46 - Say, "O Allāh, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, You will judge between your servants concerning that over which they used to differ."

deduction.title: The Listeners from the Unseen
deduction.intro: Beyond the veil of the visible world, a hidden assembly gathered in the darkness, drawn to a sound that defied all they had known. They had wandered for ages without true guidance, until a voice pierced through the silence of their unseen realm and stirred something deep within. Tonight, you are invited to step beyond the shadows and uncover the identity of this mysterious group, whose encounter with divine words led them back to their people as warners of a truth that could not be contained.
clues: We are ancient beings, existing long before many nations you have read about, yet we are rarely spoken of in human tales.
Our nature is unlike yours, woven from elements of the unseen, and we have powers that defy the ordinary.
For ages, we roamed without true guidance, until a sound reached us that stirred something deep within.
We overheard a recitation like nothing we had ever heard, words that carried a weight beyond any speech.
We pressed close in secret, commanding our own to be silent, and when the verses concluded, we could not stay still.
We returned to our people and declared: 'O our people, we have heard a Book revealed after Moses, confirming what was before it, guiding to the truth.'

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Hawariyyun (Disciples of Isa)
The Jinn who believed after hearing the Quran
The Companions of the Cave
The angels of the lowest heaven
The people of Pharaoh who followed his path
identity.answer: The Jinn who believed after hearing the Quran

trial.label: Trial / Event
options: We were cast out for refusing to bow before a newly created being
We were sealed in a cave for three hundred and nine years
We were commanded to cross a sea that split before us
We were sent to a land of magic to learn sorcery
We listened to a recitation in secret and were transformed by it
trial.answer: We listened to a recitation in secret and were transformed by it

location.label: Key Element
options: A golden calf fashioned from the ornaments of a people
A cave in which sleep lasted for generations
Two angels in Babylon who taught forbidden knowledge
A book revealed after Moses, confirming the Torah
A burning bush atop a holy mount
location.answer: A book revealed after Moses, confirming the Torah

outcome.label: Outcome
options: We were cursed until the Day of Judgment
We rose against a mighty ruler and built a barrier in the land
We were transformed into apes and swine
We returned to our people as warners, proclaiming belief
We were drowned in the sea alongside our leader
outcome.answer: We returned to our people as warners, proclaiming belief

deduction.arabic: يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلرُّشْدِ فَـَٔامَنَّا بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَن نُّشْرِكَ بِرَبِّنَآ أَحَدًۭا
deduction.verse: It guides to the right course, and we have believed in it. And we will never associate with our Lord anyone. (72:2)

translations: I swear by the spreading stars
and by the mountains as milestones
scramble.hint: This verse contains celestial imagery from a Meccan surah, describing how the heavens were stripped of their stars - a verse that invokes cosmic signs to swear by Allah's power.
